7.3.3 compact “mean ± std” table¶

In [72]:
# ============================================================
# 7.3 Compact Mean ± Std Table
# ============================================================

compact_summary = model_summary.copy()
compact_summary["AUPRC"] = compact_summary.apply(
    lambda r: f"{r['Mean_AUPRC']:.3f} ± {r['Std_AUPRC']:.3f}", axis=1
)
compact_summary["AUROC"] = compact_summary.apply(
    lambda r: f"{r['Mean_AUROC']:.3f} ± {r['Std_AUROC']:.3f}", axis=1
)

compact_summary = compact_summary[["model", "AUPRC", "AUROC"]]
display(compact_summary)
model AUPRC AUROC
1 GCN-NR-MTL 0.297 ± 0.177 0.776 ± 0.077
0 GCN-MTL 0.296 ± 0.175 0.785 ± 0.066
3 GCN-STL 0.293 ± 0.184 0.771 ± 0.076
2 GCN-SR-MTL 0.287 ± 0.158 0.793 ± 0.049

7.4 Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test¶

In [73]:
# ============================================================
# 7.4 Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test
# ============================================================

def paired_metric_df(results_df, model_a, model_b, endpoints=None, metric="AUPRC"):
    sub = results_df.copy()

    if endpoints is not None:
        sub = sub[sub["endpoint"].isin(endpoints)]

    a = sub[sub["model"] == model_a][["split", "seed", "endpoint", metric]].rename(columns={metric: f"{model_a}_{metric}"})
    b = sub[sub["model"] == model_b][["split", "seed", "endpoint", metric]].rename(columns={metric: f"{model_b}_{metric}"})

    merged = a.merge(b, on=["split", "seed", "endpoint"], how="inner").dropna()
    return merged

def run_wilcoxon(results_df, model_a, model_b, endpoints=None, metric="AUPRC"):
    paired = paired_metric_df(results_df, model_a, model_b, endpoints=endpoints, metric=metric)

    if len(paired) == 0:
        return {
            "Comparison": f"{model_a} vs {model_b}",
            "Metric": metric,
            "N": 0,
            "Statistic": np.nan,
            "p-value": np.nan
        }

    x = paired[f"{model_a}_{metric}"].values
    y = paired[f"{model_b}_{metric}"].values

    if np.allclose(x, y):
        stat, p = np.nan, 1.0
    else:
        stat, p = wilcoxon(x, y, zero_method="wilcox", alternative="two-sided")

    return {
        "Comparison": f"{model_a} vs {model_b}",
        "Metric": metric,
        "N": len(paired),
        "Statistic": stat,
        "p-value": p
    }

wilcoxon_rows = [
    run_wilcoxon(results_df, "GCN-MTL", "GCN-STL", endpoints=ALL_ENDPOINTS, metric="AUPRC"),
    run_wilcoxon(results_df, "GCN-MTL", "GCN-STL", endpoints=ALL_ENDPOINTS, metric="AUROC"),
    run_wilcoxon(results_df, "GCN-NR-MTL", "GCN-STL", endpoints=NR_ENDPOINTS, metric="AUPRC"),
    run_wilcoxon(results_df, "GCN-SR-MTL", "GCN-STL", endpoints=SR_ENDPOINTS, metric="AUPRC"),
]

wilcoxon_df = pd.DataFrame(wilcoxon_rows)

display(
    wilcoxon_df.style.format({
        "Statistic": "{:.3f}",
        "p-value": "{:.4f}"
    })
)
  Comparison Metric N Statistic p-value
0 GCN-MTL vs GCN-STL AUPRC 180 7904.000 0.7306
1 GCN-MTL vs GCN-STL AUROC 180 5180.000 0.0000
2 GCN-NR-MTL vs GCN-STL AUPRC 105 2684.000 0.7528
3 GCN-SR-MTL vs GCN-STL AUPRC 75 1115.000 0.1016

8. Transfer Analysis¶

This section analyzes transfer effects by comparing MTL against STL at the endpoint level. Transfer gain is defined as ΔAUPRC = AUPRC(MTL) − AUPRC(STL). Positive values indicate positive transfer, while negative values indicate negative transfer.

8.1 Computing Transfer Gain (ΔAUPRC = MTL − STL)¶

In [74]:
# ============================================================
# 8.1 Computing Transfer Gain (ΔAUPRC = MTL − STL)
# ============================================================

metric = "AUPRC"

stl_df = (
    results_df[results_df["model"] == "GCN-STL"]
    [["split", "seed", "endpoint", metric]]
    .rename(columns={metric: "AUPRC_STL"})
)

mtl_df = (
    results_df[results_df["model"] == "GCN-MTL"]
    [["split", "seed", "endpoint", metric]]
    .rename(columns={metric: "AUPRC_MTL"})
)

transfer_df = stl_df.merge(
    mtl_df,
    on=["split", "seed", "endpoint"],
    how="inner"
)

transfer_df["TransferGain"] = transfer_df["AUPRC_MTL"] - transfer_df["AUPRC_STL"]
transfer_df["Group"] = transfer_df["endpoint"].apply(
    lambda x: "NR" if x in NR_ENDPOINTS else "SR"
)

display(transfer_df.head())
split seed endpoint AUPRC_STL AUPRC_MTL TransferGain Group
0 0 0 NR-AR 0.277993 0.203086 -0.074907 NR
1 0 0 NR-AR-LBD 0.028678 0.036832 0.008154 NR
2 0 0 NR-AhR 0.566122 0.544451 -0.021670 NR
3 0 0 NR-Aromatase 0.130767 0.168236 0.037469 NR
4 0 0 NR-ER 0.254902 0.381261 0.126359 NR

8.2 Endpoint-Level Transfer Classification¶

In [75]:
# ============================================================
# 8.2 Endpoint-Level Transfer Classification
# ============================================================

endpoint_transfer = (
    transfer_df
    .groupby(["endpoint", "Group"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_TransferGain=("TransferGain", "mean"),
        Std_TransferGain=("TransferGain", "std"),
        Mean_AUPRC_STL=("AUPRC_STL", "mean"),
        Mean_AUPRC_MTL=("AUPRC_MTL", "mean")
    )
)

def classify_transfer(x):
    if x > 0:
        return "Positive transfer"
    elif x < 0:
        return "Negative transfer"
    else:
        return "No change"

endpoint_transfer["TransferType"] = endpoint_transfer["Mean_TransferGain"].apply(classify_transfer)

endpoint_transfer = endpoint_transfer.sort_values("Mean_TransferGain").reset_index(drop=True)

display(
    endpoint_transfer.style.format({
        "Mean_TransferGain": "{:.3f}",
        "Std_TransferGain": "{:.3f}",
        "Mean_AUPRC_STL": "{:.3f}",
        "Mean_AUPRC_MTL": "{:.3f}"
    })
)
  endpoint Group Mean_TransferGain Std_TransferGain Mean_AUPRC_STL Mean_AUPRC_MTL TransferType
0 NR-AhR NR -0.038 0.056 0.498 0.460 Negative transfer
1 SR-MMP SR -0.031 0.044 0.576 0.546 Negative transfer
2 NR-ER-LBD NR -0.027 0.051 0.294 0.267 Negative transfer
3 SR-ARE SR -0.018 0.045 0.399 0.381 Negative transfer
4 NR-Aromatase NR -0.007 0.033 0.173 0.166 Negative transfer
5 SR-ATAD5 SR 0.000 0.025 0.128 0.128 Positive transfer
6 NR-PPAR-gamma NR 0.006 0.045 0.093 0.099 Positive transfer
7 SR-HSE SR 0.011 0.043 0.195 0.206 Positive transfer
8 SR-p53 SR 0.014 0.036 0.187 0.202 Positive transfer
9 NR-AR-LBD NR 0.031 0.064 0.266 0.297 Positive transfer
10 NR-AR NR 0.046 0.096 0.350 0.395 Positive transfer
11 NR-ER NR 0.049 0.057 0.356 0.405 Positive transfer

8.3 NR vs SR Group-Level Analysis¶

In [76]:
# ============================================================
# 8.3 NR vs SR Group-Level Analysis
# ============================================================

group_transfer = (
    endpoint_transfer
    .groupby("Group", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_TransferGain=("Mean_TransferGain", "mean"),
        Median_TransferGain=("Mean_TransferGain", "median"),
        Positive_Endpoints=("TransferType", lambda x: (x == "Positive transfer").sum()),
        Negative_Endpoints=("TransferType", lambda x: (x == "Negative transfer").sum()),
        Total_Endpoints=("endpoint", "count")
    )
)

display(
    group_transfer.style.format({
        "Mean_TransferGain": "{:.3f}",
        "Median_TransferGain": "{:.3f}"
    })
)
  Group Mean_TransferGain Median_TransferGain Positive_Endpoints Negative_Endpoints Total_Endpoints
0 NR 0.009 0.006 4 3 7
1 SR -0.005 0.000 3 2 5

8.4 Transfer Gain vs Phi Similarity¶

In [79]:
# ============================================================
# 8.4 Transfer Gain vs Phi Similarity (FIXED)
# ============================================================

# Step 1: convert FIRST
phi_matrix = pd.DataFrame(
    phi_matrix,
    index=ALL_ENDPOINTS,
    columns=ALL_ENDPOINTS
)

# Step 2: compute mean phi
phi_mean_rows = []

for endpoint in ALL_ENDPOINTS:
    other_eps = [e for e in ALL_ENDPOINTS if e != endpoint]
    mean_phi = phi_matrix.loc[endpoint, other_eps].mean()

    phi_mean_rows.append({
        "endpoint": endpoint,
        "MeanPhiToOthers": mean_phi
    })

phi_mean_df = pd.DataFrame(phi_mean_rows)

8.5 Transfer Gain vs Sparsity and Imbalance¶

In [80]:
# ============================================================
# 8.5 Transfer Gain vs Sparsity and Imbalance
# ============================================================

# From Section 1.4
coverage_df = missing_df[["Endpoint", "Coverage"]].rename(columns={"Endpoint": "endpoint"})

# From Section 1.5
imbalance_summary_df = imbalance_df[["Endpoint", "Positive rate"]].rename(columns={"Endpoint": "endpoint"})

endpoint_transfer = endpoint_transfer.merge(coverage_df, on="endpoint", how="left")
endpoint_transfer = endpoint_transfer.merge(imbalance_summary_df, on="endpoint", how="left")

corr_coverage = endpoint_transfer["Mean_TransferGain"].corr(endpoint_transfer["Coverage"])
corr_posrate  = endpoint_transfer["Mean_TransferGain"].corr(endpoint_transfer["Positive rate"])

transfer_factor_summary = endpoint_transfer[[
    "endpoint", "Group", "Mean_TransferGain", "Coverage", "Positive rate", "TransferType"
]].sort_values("Mean_TransferGain")

display(
    transfer_factor_summary.style.format({
        "Mean_TransferGain": "{:.3f}",
        "Coverage": "{:.3f}",
        "Positive rate": "{:.3f}"
    })
)

print(f"Correlation between transfer gain and label coverage: {corr_coverage:.3f}")
print(f"Correlation between transfer gain and positive rate: {corr_posrate:.3f}")
  endpoint Group Mean_TransferGain Coverage Positive rate TransferType
0 NR-AhR NR -0.038 0.835 0.119 Negative transfer
1 SR-MMP SR -0.031 0.740 0.159 Negative transfer
2 NR-ER-LBD NR -0.027 0.888 0.051 Negative transfer
3 SR-ARE SR -0.018 0.744 0.161 Negative transfer
4 NR-Aromatase NR -0.007 0.739 0.050 Negative transfer
5 SR-ATAD5 SR 0.000 0.902 0.038 Positive transfer
6 NR-PPAR-gamma NR 0.006 0.822 0.029 Positive transfer
7 SR-HSE SR 0.011 0.825 0.057 Positive transfer
8 SR-p53 SR 0.014 0.865 0.063 Positive transfer
9 NR-AR-LBD NR 0.031 0.862 0.036 Positive transfer
10 NR-AR NR 0.046 0.926 0.043 Positive transfer
11 NR-ER NR 0.049 0.791 0.129 Positive transfer 
Correlation between transfer gain and label coverage: 0.347
Correlation between transfer gain and positive rate: -0.367
In [81]:
# ============================================================
# Transfer Gain Plot
# ============================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

plot_df = endpoint_transfer.sort_values("Mean_TransferGain").copy()

colors = plot_df["Group"].map({"NR": "#9BBFE5", "SR": "#F7B799"})

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.8))

ax.bar(
    plot_df["endpoint"],
    plot_df["Mean_TransferGain"],
    color=colors,
    edgecolor="none"
)

ax.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)

ax.set_ylabel("ΔAUPRC (MTL − STL)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Endpoint-Level Transfer Gain", fontsize=13, weight="bold")
ax.set_xticklabels(plot_df["endpoint"], rotation=40, ha="right", fontsize=10)

ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

# legend
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
legend_handles = [
    Patch(facecolor="#9BBFE5", label="NR"),
    Patch(facecolor="#F7B799", label="SR")
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_handles, frameon=True, fontsize=10)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

9. Visualization¶

9.1 Baseline Comparison Plots¶

In [92]:
# ============================================================
# 9.1 Baseline Comparison Plots
# ============================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from matplotlib.patches import Patch

plot_df = baseline_results.pivot(index="Endpoint", columns="Model", values="AUPRC")
plot_df = plot_df.loc[ALL_ENDPOINTS]

x = np.arange(len(plot_df.index))
width = 0.36

colors = {
    "Logistic Regression": "#9BBFE5",
    "Random Forest": "#F7B799"
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

ax.bar(
    x - width / 2,
    plot_df["Logistic Regression"],
    width=width,
    label="Logistic Regression",
    color=colors["Logistic Regression"]
)

ax.bar(
    x + width / 2,
    plot_df["Random Forest"],
    width=width,
    label="Random Forest",
    color=colors["Random Forest"]
)

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(plot_df.index, rotation=40, ha="right", fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylabel("Test AUPRC", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Baseline STL Performance on Tox21", fontsize=13, weight="bold")

nr_end = len(NR_ENDPOINTS) - 1
ax.axvline(nr_end + 0.5, linestyle="--", color="grey", alpha=0.5)

ylim_top = ax.get_ylim()[1]
ax.text(nr_end / 2, ylim_top * 0.95, "NR", ha="center", fontsize=11, weight="bold", color="grey")
ax.text(nr_end + 1 + (len(SR_ENDPOINTS) - 1) / 2, ylim_top * 0.95, "SR", ha="center", fontsize=11, weight="bold", color="grey")

ax.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=10)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [83]:
# ============================================================
# 9.2 STL vs MTL Comparison
# ============================================================

# Build endpoint-level STL/MTL summary if not already available
stl_mtl_compare = (
    results_df[results_df["model"].isin(["GCN-STL", "GCN-MTL"])]
    .groupby(["model", "endpoint"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std")
    )
)

plot_df = stl_mtl_compare.pivot(index="endpoint", columns="model", values="Mean_AUPRC")
plot_df = plot_df.loc[ALL_ENDPOINTS]

x = np.arange(len(plot_df.index))
width = 0.36

colors = {
    "GCN-STL": "#A8D5BA",
    "GCN-MTL": "#F4B6C2"
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

ax.bar(
    x - width / 2,
    plot_df["GCN-STL"],
    width=width,
    label="GCN-STL",
    color=colors["GCN-STL"]
)

ax.bar(
    x + width / 2,
    plot_df["GCN-MTL"],
    width=width,
    label="GCN-MTL",
    color=colors["GCN-MTL"]
)

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(plot_df.index, rotation=40, ha="right", fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylabel("Mean Test AUPRC", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("GCN-STL vs GCN-MTL by Endpoint", fontsize=13, weight="bold")

nr_end = len(NR_ENDPOINTS) - 1
ax.axvline(nr_end + 0.5, linestyle="--", color="grey", alpha=0.5)

ylim_top = ax.get_ylim()[1]
ax.text(nr_end / 2, ylim_top * 0.95, "NR", ha="center", fontsize=11, weight="bold", color="grey")
ax.text(nr_end + 1 + (len(SR_ENDPOINTS) - 1) / 2, ylim_top * 0.95, "SR", ha="center", fontsize=11, weight="bold", color="grey")

ax.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=10)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [84]:
# ============================================================
# 9.3 Group-Level Bar Plots
# ============================================================

# If not available, rebuild from endpoint_transfer
if "group_transfer" not in globals():
    group_transfer = (
        endpoint_transfer
        .groupby("Group", as_index=False)
        .agg(
            Mean_TransferGain=("Mean_TransferGain", "mean"),
            Median_TransferGain=("Mean_TransferGain", "median"),
            Positive_Endpoints=("TransferType", lambda x: (x == "Positive transfer").sum()),
            Negative_Endpoints=("TransferType", lambda x: (x == "Negative transfer").sum()),
            Total_Endpoints=("endpoint", "count")
        )
    )

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.5, 4.5))

bars = ax.bar(
    group_transfer["Group"],
    group_transfer["Mean_TransferGain"],
    color=["#9BBFE5", "#F7B799"],
    width=0.55
)

ax.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)
ax.set_ylabel("Mean ΔAUPRC", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Group-Level Transfer Gain", fontsize=13, weight="bold")

ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [85]:
# ============================================================
# 9.4 Error Bars (Mean ± Std)
# ============================================================

# Prepare endpoint summary if needed
if "endpoint_summary" not in globals():
    endpoint_summary = (
        results_df
        .groupby(["model", "endpoint", "group"], as_index=False)
        .agg(
            Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
            Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std")
        )
    )

plot_df = endpoint_summary[endpoint_summary["model"].isin(["GCN-STL", "GCN-MTL"])].copy()

stl_df = plot_df[plot_df["model"] == "GCN-STL"].set_index("endpoint").loc[ALL_ENDPOINTS]
mtl_df = plot_df[plot_df["model"] == "GCN-MTL"].set_index("endpoint").loc[ALL_ENDPOINTS]

x = np.arange(len(ALL_ENDPOINTS))
width = 0.36

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

ax.bar(
    x - width / 2,
    stl_df["Mean_AUPRC"],
    width=width,
    yerr=stl_df["Std_AUPRC"],
    capsize=3,
    color="#A8D5BA",
    label="GCN-STL"
)

ax.bar(
    x + width / 2,
    mtl_df["Mean_AUPRC"],
    width=width,
    yerr=mtl_df["Std_AUPRC"],
    capsize=3,
    color="#F4B6C2",
    label="GCN-MTL"
)

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(ALL_ENDPOINTS, rotation=40, ha="right", fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylabel("AUPRC (Mean ± Std)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Endpoint-Level Performance with Error Bars", fontsize=13, weight="bold")

ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(frameon=True, fontsize=10)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [ ]:
# ============================================================
# 9.5 Endpoint-Level Transfer Gain
# ============================================================

plot_df = endpoint_transfer.sort_values("Mean_TransferGain").copy()

group_colors = {
    "NR": "#9BBFE5",
    "SR": "#F7B799"
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.8))

ax.bar(
    plot_df["endpoint"],
    plot_df["Mean_TransferGain"],
    color=plot_df["Group"].map(group_colors),
    edgecolor="none"
)

ax.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)
ax.set_ylabel("ΔAUPRC (MTL − STL)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Endpoint-Level Transfer Gain", fontsize=13, weight="bold")
ax.set_xticklabels(plot_df["endpoint"], rotation=40, ha="right", fontsize=10)

ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

legend_handles = [
    Patch(facecolor="#9BBFE5", label="NR"),
    Patch(facecolor="#F7B799", label="SR")
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_handles, frameon=True, fontsize=10)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [97]:
# ============================================================
# Violin + Box Hybrid Plot (Publication Style)
# ============================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd

seed_plot_df = (
    results_df
    .groupby(["model", "split", "seed"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Mean_AUROC=("AUROC", "mean")
    )
)

order = ["GCN-STL", "GCN-MTL", "GCN-NR-MTL", "GCN-SR-MTL"]

colors = {
    "GCN-STL": "#A8D5BA",
    "GCN-MTL": "#F4B6C2",
    "GCN-NR-MTL": "#9BBFE5",
    "GCN-SR-MTL": "#F7B799"
}
palette = [colors[m] for m in order]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5), dpi=140)

for ax, metric, title in zip(
    axes,
    ["Mean_AUPRC", "Mean_AUROC"],
    ["AUPRC", "AUROC"]
):
    sns.violinplot(
        data=seed_plot_df,
        x="model",
        y=metric,
        order=order,
        palette=palette,
        inner=None,
        cut=0,
        linewidth=0,
        ax=ax
    )

    sns.boxplot(
        data=seed_plot_df,
        x="model",
        y=metric,
        order=order,
        width=0.25,
        showcaps=True,
        boxprops={'facecolor':'white', 'zorder':3},
        showfliers=False,
        whiskerprops={'linewidth':1},
        ax=ax
    )

    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=13, weight="bold")
    ax.set_xlabel("")
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=20)
    ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)

    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)

fig.suptitle("Model Stability Across Splits and Seeds", fontsize=15)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [105]:
# ============================================================
# Paired comparison plot by group (NR / SR): AUPRC + AUROC
# ============================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# ----------------------------
# 1. Keep only relevant models and groups
# ----------------------------
plot_df = results_df.copy()

def map_model_family(model):
    if model == "GCN-STL":
        return "STL"
    elif model == "GCN-MTL":
        return "Full MTL"
    elif model in ["GCN-NR-MTL", "GCN-SR-MTL"]:
        return "Grouped MTL"
    else:
        return model

plot_df["ModelFamily"] = plot_df["model"].apply(map_model_family)

# Keep only meaningful combinations
plot_df = plot_df[
    ((plot_df["group"] == "NR") & (plot_df["model"].isin(["GCN-STL", "GCN-MTL", "GCN-NR-MTL"]))) |
    ((plot_df["group"] == "SR") & (plot_df["model"].isin(["GCN-STL", "GCN-MTL", "GCN-SR-MTL"])))
].copy()

# ----------------------------
# 2. Aggregate to one value per run
# ----------------------------
paired_df = (
    plot_df
    .groupby(["split", "seed", "group", "ModelFamily"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        AUROC=("AUROC", "mean")
    )
)

paired_auprc = paired_df.pivot_table(
    index=["split", "seed", "group"],
    columns="ModelFamily",
    values="AUPRC"
).reset_index()

paired_auroc = paired_df.pivot_table(
    index=["split", "seed", "group"],
    columns="ModelFamily",
    values="AUROC"
).reset_index()

# ----------------------------
# 3. Plot settings
# ----------------------------
group_colors = {
    "NR": "#6BA292",
    "SR": "#E5989B"
}

model_order = ["STL", "Full MTL", "Grouped MTL"]
x_positions = [0, 1, 2]

TITLE_SIZE = 16
PANEL_TITLE_SIZE = 13
AXIS_LABEL_SIZE = 11
TICK_SIZE = 10
ANNOT_SIZE = 9

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(11, 8), dpi=150, sharex=True)

# axes:
# [0,0] NR AUPRC
# [0,1] SR AUPRC
# [1,0] NR AUROC
# [1,1] SR AUROC

panel_map = [
    (axes[0, 0], paired_auprc, "NR", "AUPRC"),
    (axes[0, 1], paired_auprc, "SR", "AUPRC"),
    (axes[1, 0], paired_auroc, "NR", "AUROC"),
    (axes[1, 1], paired_auroc, "SR", "AUROC"),
]

for ax, df_metric, group, metric_name in panel_map:
    sub = df_metric[df_metric["group"] == group].copy()

    # paired lines for repeated runs
    for _, row in sub.iterrows():
        yvals = [row[m] for m in model_order]
        ax.plot(
            x_positions,
            yvals,
            color=group_colors[group],
            alpha=0.28,
            linewidth=1
        )
        ax.scatter(
            x_positions,
            yvals,
            color=group_colors[group],
            s=16,
            alpha=0.75
        )

    # mean trend
    mean_vals = [sub[m].mean() for m in model_order]
    ax.plot(
        x_positions,
        mean_vals,
        color="black",
        linewidth=2.2,
        marker="o",
        markersize=4.5,
        label="Mean"
    )

    # mean labels
    for x, y in zip(x_positions, mean_vals):
        ax.text(
            x,
            y + (0.004 if metric_name == "AUPRC" else 0.003),
            f"{y:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=ANNOT_SIZE
        )

    ax.set_title(f"{group} — {metric_name}", fontsize=PANEL_TITLE_SIZE, weight="bold")
    ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.25)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.tick_params(axis="both", labelsize=TICK_SIZE)

# x-axis labels on bottom row
for ax in axes[1, :]:
    ax.set_xticks(x_positions)
    ax.set_xticklabels(model_order, rotation=15, fontsize=TICK_SIZE)

# also show top row labels for readability
for ax in axes[0, :]:
    ax.set_xticks(x_positions)
    ax.set_xticklabels(model_order, rotation=15, fontsize=TICK_SIZE)

axes[0, 0].set_ylabel("Mean AUPRC per run", fontsize=AXIS_LABEL_SIZE)
axes[1, 0].set_ylabel("Mean AUROC per run", fontsize=AXIS_LABEL_SIZE)

axes[0, 1].legend(frameon=True, fontsize=9, loc="best")

fig.suptitle(
    "Paired Comparison Across Repeated Runs: NR vs SR",
    fontsize=TITLE_SIZE,
    weight="bold",
    y=0.98
)

plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.96])
plt.show()

Interestingly, we observe divergent trends between AUPRC and AUROC on SR endpoints. While AUROC improves with multi-task learning, AUPRC slightly decreases.

This suggests that multi-task learning improves the global ranking of predictions, but does not translate into better precision-recall performance under severe class imbalance. In other words, although positive samples are ranked higher on average, the model produces more false positives, reducing precision.

This highlights the importance of evaluating both AUROC and AUPRC, especially for imbalanced toxicity datasets such as Tox21.

In [108]:
# ============================================================
# Clean comparison: NR vs SR (AUPRC + AUROC)
# ============================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# ----------------------------
# Aggregate
# ----------------------------
summary_df = (
    results_df
    .groupby(["group", "model"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        AUPRC_mean=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        AUPRC_std=("AUPRC", "std"),
        AUROC_mean=("AUROC", "mean"),
        AUROC_std=("AUROC", "std")
    )
)

# Map model names
model_map = {
    "GCN-STL": "STL",
    "GCN-MTL": "Full MTL",
    "GCN-NR-MTL": "Grouped MTL",
    "GCN-SR-MTL": "Grouped MTL"
}

summary_df["Model"] = summary_df["model"].map(model_map)

# remove duplicates (NR-MTL vs SR-MTL) using numeric columns only
summary_df = (
    summary_df
    .groupby(["group", "Model"], as_index=False)[
        ["AUPRC_mean", "AUPRC_std", "AUROC_mean", "AUROC_std"]
    ]
    .mean()
)

# ----------------------------
# Plot
# ----------------------------
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 4.5), dpi=150)

colors = {
    "STL": "#6BA292",
    "Full MTL": "#E5989B",
    "Grouped MTL": "#6C91BF"
}

model_order = ["STL", "Full MTL", "Grouped MTL"]
x = np.arange(len(model_order))

for ax, metric, title in zip(
    axes,
    ["AUPRC", "AUROC"],
    ["AUPRC", "AUROC"]
):
    for group, shift in zip(["NR", "SR"], [-0.08, 0.08]):
        sub = summary_df[summary_df["group"] == group].copy()
        sub = sub.set_index("Model").loc[model_order]

        means = sub[f"{metric}_mean"]
        stds = sub[f"{metric}_std"]

        ax.errorbar(
            x + shift,
            means,
            yerr=stds,
            fmt="o",
            capsize=3,
            label=group if metric == "AUPRC" else "",
            color="#333333",
            ecolor="#333333",
            elinewidth=1,
            markeredgewidth=0
        )

        for i, m in enumerate(model_order):
            ax.scatter(
                x[i] + shift,
                means.iloc[i],
                color=colors[m],
                s=60,
                zorder=3
            )

    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(model_order, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=13, weight="bold")
    ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.3)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)

axes[0].set_ylabel("Score", fontsize=11)
axes[0].legend(title="Group", fontsize=9)

fig.suptitle("NR vs SR Performance Comparison", fontsize=15, weight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

10. Final Tables and Reporting¶

10.1 Endpoint-Level Results Table¶

# ============================================================
# 10.1 Endpoint-Level Results Table
# ============================================================

endpoint_results_table = (
    results_df
    .groupby(["model", "endpoint", "group"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std"),
        Mean_AUROC=("AUROC", "mean"),
        Std_AUROC=("AUROC", "std")
    )
    .sort_values(["model", "endpoint"])
)

display(
    endpoint_results_table.style.format({
        "Mean_AUPRC": "{:.3f}",
        "Std_AUPRC": "{:.3f}",
        "Mean_AUROC": "{:.3f}",
        "Std_AUROC": "{:.3f}"
    })
)

group_summary_table = (
    results_df
    .groupby(["model", "group"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std"),

)

)

In [89]:
# ============================================================
# 10.3 Leaderboard Comparison Table
# ============================================================

# Baseline summary
baseline_model_summary = (
    baseline_results
    .groupby("Model", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Mean_AUROC=("AUROC", "mean")
    )
    .rename(columns={"Model": "model"})
)

baseline_model_summary["Std_AUPRC"] = np.nan
baseline_model_summary["Std_AUROC"] = np.nan

# GNN summary
gnn_model_summary = (
    results_df
    .groupby("model", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std"),
        Mean_AUROC=("AUROC", "mean"),
        Std_AUROC=("AUROC", "std")
    )
)

leaderboard_df = pd.concat(
    [baseline_model_summary, gnn_model_summary],
    ignore_index=True
)

leaderboard_df["AUPRC"] = leaderboard_df.apply(
    lambda r: f"{r['Mean_AUPRC']:.3f}" if pd.isna(r["Std_AUPRC"])
    else f"{r['Mean_AUPRC']:.3f} ± {r['Std_AUPRC']:.3f}",
    axis=1
)

leaderboard_df["AUROC"] = leaderboard_df.apply(
    lambda r: f"{r['Mean_AUROC']:.3f}" if pd.isna(r["Std_AUROC"])
    else f"{r['Mean_AUROC']:.3f} ± {r['Std_AUROC']:.3f}",
    axis=1
)

leaderboard_df = leaderboard_df[["model", "AUPRC", "AUROC"]].sort_values("model")

display(leaderboard_df)
model AUPRC AUROC
2 GCN-MTL 0.296 ± 0.175 0.785 ± 0.066
3 GCN-NR-MTL 0.297 ± 0.177 0.776 ± 0.077
4 GCN-SR-MTL 0.287 ± 0.158 0.793 ± 0.049
5 GCN-STL 0.293 ± 0.184 0.771 ± 0.076
0 Logistic Regression 0.291 0.728
1 Random Forest 0.337 0.771

# 10.4 Export of Final Results
# ============================================================

endpoint_results_table.to_csv("endpoint_results_table.csv", index=False)
group_summary_table.to_csv("group_summary_table.csv", index=False)
leaderboard_df.to_csv("leaderboard_comparison_table.csv", index=False)

print("Final result tables exported successfully:")
print("- endpoint_results_table.csv")
print("- group_summary_table.csv")
print("- leaderboard_comparison_table.csv")

In [110]:
# ============================================================
# Table X: Our Results (Scaffold split, repeated runs)
# ============================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# ----------------------------
# Aggregate your results
# ----------------------------
our_table = (
    results_df
    .groupby("model", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        Mean_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "mean"),
        Std_AUPRC=("AUPRC", "std"),
        Mean_AUROC=("AUROC", "mean"),
        Std_AUROC=("AUROC", "std")
    )
)

# ----------------------------
# Pretty names
# ----------------------------
name_map = {
    "GCN-STL": "GCN-STL",
    "GCN-MTL": "GCN-MTL",
    "GCN-NR-MTL": "GCN-NR-MTL",
    "GCN-SR-MTL": "GCN-SR-MTL",
    "Logistic Regression": "Logistic Regression",
    "Random Forest": "Random Forest"
}

our_table["Model"] = our_table["model"].map(name_map)

# ----------------------------
# Format mean ± std
# ----------------------------
def fmt(mean, std):
    return f"{mean:.3f} ± {std:.3f}"

our_table["AUPRC"] = our_table.apply(lambda r: fmt(r["Mean_AUPRC"], r["Std_AUPRC"]), axis=1)
our_table["AUROC"] = our_table.apply(lambda r: fmt(r["Mean_AUROC"], r["Std_AUROC"]), axis=1)

# ----------------------------
# Select final columns
# ----------------------------
our_table_final = our_table[["Model", "AUROC", "AUPRC"]].copy()

display(our_table_final)
Model AUROC AUPRC
0 GCN-MTL 0.785 ± 0.066 0.296 ± 0.175
1 GCN-NR-MTL 0.776 ± 0.077 0.297 ± 0.177
2 GCN-SR-MTL 0.793 ± 0.049 0.287 ± 0.158
3 GCN-STL 0.771 ± 0.076 0.293 ± 0.184

# ============================================================
# Table Y: Published reference results
# ============================================================

ref_data = [
    ["MoleculeNet (2017)", "KernelSVM", "Random split", 0.822, None, "Best conventional model"],
    ["MoleculeNet (2017)", "Graph Convolution (GC)", "Random split", 0.829, None, "Best graph model"],

    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "SVM", "Reported split", 0.826, None, "Descriptor model"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "XGBoost", "Reported split", 0.847, None, "Strong baseline"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "Random Forest", "Reported split", 0.858, None, "Best in study"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "DNN", "Reported split", 0.854, None, "Feed-forward NN"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "GCN", "Reported split", 0.837, None, "Graph model"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "GAT", "Reported split", 0.830, None, "Attention model"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "MPNN", "Reported split", 0.802, None, "Message passing"],
    ["Jiang et al. (2021)", "Attentive FP", "Reported split", 0.847, None, "Strong graph model"],

    ["Born et al. (2023)", "Random Forest (ECFP4)", "Score dataset", 0.782, None, "Baseline"],
    ["Born et al. (2023)", "GCN", "Score dataset", 0.828, None, "Best model"]
]

ref_table = pd.DataFrame(ref_data, columns=[
    "Source", "Model", "Split", "AUROC", "AUPRC", "Notes"
])

# Format AUROC
ref_table["AUROC"] = ref_table["AUROC"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.3f}")

display(ref_table)
Source Model Split AUROC AUPRC Notes
0 MoleculeNet (2017) KernelSVM Random split 0.822 None Best conventional model
1 MoleculeNet (2017) Graph Convolution (GC) Random split 0.829 None Best graph model
2 Jiang et al. (2021) SVM Reported split 0.826 None Descriptor model
3 Jiang et al. (2021) XGBoost Reported split 0.847 None Strong baseline
4 Jiang et al. (2021) Random Forest Reported split 0.858 None Best in study
5 Jiang et al. (2021) DNN Reported split 0.854 None Feed-forward NN
6 Jiang et al. (2021) GCN Reported split 0.837 None Graph model
7 Jiang et al. (2021) GAT Reported split 0.830 None Attention model
8 Jiang et al. (2021) MPNN Reported split 0.802 None Message passing
9 Jiang et al. (2021) Attentive FP Reported split 0.847 None Strong graph model
10 Born et al. (2023) Random Forest (ECFP4) Score dataset 0.782 None Baseline
11 Born et al. (2023) GCN Score dataset 0.828 None Best model
In [1]:
# ============================================================
# Final Summary
# ============================================================

print("=== FINAL INSIGHTS ===")

print("- Random Forest is a strong baseline under scaffold split")
print("- MTL provides limited average improvement")
print("- Negative transfer occurs in several endpoints")
print("- SR tasks benefit more from MTL than NR tasks")

print("\nConclusion: MTL is not universally beneficial and depends on task structure and data quality.")

=== FINAL INSIGHTS ===
- Random Forest is a strong baseline under scaffold split
- MTL provides limited average improvement
- Negative transfer occurs in several endpoints
- SR tasks benefit more from MTL than NR tasks

Conclusion: MTL is not universally beneficial and depends on task structure and data quality.
